meta.title: PageContent
meta.description: A layout component that constrains page content width and arranges content in single or multi-column layouts. It consolidates multiple width-based containers into a single compound component with explicit column control.

mdx:
## Overview

PageContent provides a CSS grid-based centering pattern with configurable
max-width constraints and column layouts. It supports three width variants
(wide, narrow, full) and three column configurations (single, equal split, and
2:1 ratio) with built-in responsive collapse for multi-column layouts.

### Key features

- **Width constraints**: Three variants control the max-width of the content
  area
- **Column layouts**: Single, equal-split (1/1), and sidebar (2/1) column
  configurations
- **Responsive collapse**: Multi-column layouts collapse to a single column on
  smaller screens
- **Sticky sidebar**: Column-level sticky positioning for sidebar patterns
- **Customizable gap**: Default spacing between columns can be overridden

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Merchant Center Page Content Layouts](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Width variants

The `variant` prop controls the max-width constraint of the content area.

#### Wide

The default variant. Content is constrained to a wide max-width, suitable for
large forms or tables with few columns.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide">
    <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Wide</Text>
      <Text>Constrained to a wide max-width. Best for large forms or tables with few columns.</Text>
    </Box>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

#### Narrow

Content is constrained to a narrower max-width, suitable for simple forms,
collapsible panels, and cards.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="narrow">
    <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Narrow</Text>
      <Text>Constrained to a narrow max-width. Best for simple forms and focused content.</Text>
      <Text color="neutral.11">The difference from wide is only visible when the viewport is wider than the narrow max-width of 3xl (768px).</Text>
    </Box>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

#### Full

Content spans the full available width with no max-width constraint. Suitable
for large tables and content with a horizontal reading flow.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="full">
    <Box bg="teal.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" color="teal.12">Full</Text>
      <Text>No width constraint. Best for large tables and horizontal reading flows.</Text>
    </Box>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

### Column layouts

The `columns` prop controls how content is arranged within the container. Use
`PageContent.Column` to wrap each column's content.

#### Single column

The default layout. Content flows in a single column. No `PageContent.Column`
wrappers are needed.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1">
    <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Single column</Text>
      <Text>Content flows in a single, full-width column.</Text>
    </Box>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

#### Equal columns (1/1)

Two columns of equal width. Best for grouping related content side by side to
save vertical space.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Left column</Text>
        <Text>Equal width with the right column.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Right column</Text>
        <Text>Equal width with the left column.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

#### Sidebar layout (2/1)

A wider main column with a narrower sidebar column. Best for forms with meta
information or contextual links.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content (2fr)</Text>
        <Text>The wider column for primary content such as forms.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sidebar (1fr)</Text>
        <Text>Narrower column for meta information.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

### Sticky sidebar

Use the `sticky` prop on a `PageContent.Column` to make it stick to the top of
the viewport during scroll. This is commonly used for sidebar navigation or meta
information that should remain visible.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200" minHeight="300px">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content</Text>
        <Text>Tall content area that creates scrollable space.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column sticky>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sticky sidebar</Text>
        <Text>This column stays visible while scrolling.</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

### Custom gap

The default gap between columns is `800` (32px). Override it with the `gap`
style prop for tighter or wider spacing.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1">
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Default gap (800)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12">32px between columns</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1" gap="1600">
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Large gap (1600)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12">64px between columns</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

## Specs

<PropsTable id="PageContent" />

## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone.

### Accessibility standards

- **Semantic structure:** PageContent renders semantic `<div>` elements that
  provide a logical content structure for assistive technologies.
- **Reading order:** The DOM order matches the visual order, ensuring screen
  readers present content in the expected sequence.
- **Responsive reflow:** Multi-column layouts collapse to a single column on
  smaller screens, maintaining readability and preventing horizontal scrolling
  (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow).
- **Landmark usage:** PageContent is a layout primitive. Wrap content in
  appropriate landmark elements (`<main>`, `<aside>`, `<section>`) to provide
  additional context for screen readers.

## Predecessor

PageContent replaces the `PageContentWide`, `PageContentNarrow`, and
`PageContentFull` components from Merchant Center Application Kit. For detailed
migration instructions, see the
[Migration guide](/components/layout/pagecontent/dev#migrating-from-merchant-center-application-kit)
in the implementation docs. For the original Application Kit documentation, see
[Page Content Layouts](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts).

overview.mdx:
## Overview

PageContent provides a CSS grid-based centering pattern with configurable
max-width constraints and column layouts. It supports three width variants
(wide, narrow, full) and three column configurations (single, equal split, and
2:1 ratio) with built-in responsive collapse for multi-column layouts.

### Key features

- **Width constraints**: Three variants control the max-width of the content

- **Column layouts**: Single, equal-split (1/1), and sidebar (2/1) column
  configurations
- **Responsive collapse**: Multi-column layouts collapse to a single column on
  smaller screens
- **Sticky sidebar**: Column-level sticky positioning for sidebar patterns
- **Customizable gap**: Default spacing between columns can be overridden

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Merchant Center Page Content Layouts](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts)

Get familiar with the features.

#### Narrow

#### Single column


dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { PageContent, type PageContentProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus';
```

### Basic usage

The simplest usage is a single-column width-constrained layout. No `PageContent.Column` wrappers are needed for single-column content.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide">
    <Text>This content is constrained to the wide max-width.</Text>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

## Usage examples

### Width variants

The `variant` prop controls the max-width constraint. Available values are `wide`, `narrow`, and `full`.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="800">
   <PageContent.Root variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="purple.12" fontWeight="bold">narrow</Text>
        <Text color="neutral.11">The difference from wide is only visible when the viewport is wider than the narrow max-width of 3xl (768px).</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="wide">
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="blue.12" fontWeight="bold">wide</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="full">
      <Box bg="teal.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="teal.12" fontWeight="bold">full</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Multi-column layouts

Use the `columns` prop with `PageContent.Column` wrappers for multi-column layouts.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="800" >
    <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1">
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12" fontWeight="bold">Left (equal)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12" fontWeight="bold">Right (equal)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12" fontWeight="bold">Main content (2fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12" fontWeight="bold">Sidebar (1fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Sticky sidebar

Use the `sticky` prop on a `PageContent.Column` to make it stick during scroll. Override the default `top: 0` with the `top` style prop.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1" maxHeight="250px" overflowY="auto">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200" minHeight="300px">
        <Text color="blue.12" fontWeight="bold">Main content area (tall)</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column sticky top="400">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="purple.12" fontWeight="bold">Sticky sidebar</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

### Custom gap

The default gap between columns is `800` (32px). Override with the `gap` style prop.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1" gap="1600">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="blue.12" fontWeight="bold">Column 1</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text color="purple.12" fontWeight="bold">Column 2</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

## API reference

<PropsTable id="PageContent" />

## Testing your implementation

These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using PageContent within your application.

### Usage Examples

Consumer implementation patterns for PageContent

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { PageContent, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("PageContent - Usage examples", () => {
  it("renders a 2/1 layout with sticky sidebar", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
          <PageContent.Column>Main content</PageContent.Column>
          <PageContent.Column sticky>Sidebar</PageContent.Column>
        </PageContent.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByText("Main content")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Sidebar")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```


## Migrating from Merchant Center Application Kit

`PageContent` replaces the three separate page content containers from
[`@commercetools-frontend/application-components`](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts):

| Application Kit Component | Nimbus Equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<PageContentWide>` | `<PageContent.Root variant="wide">` |
| `<PageContentNarrow>` | `<PageContent.Root variant="narrow">` |
| `<PageContentFull>` | `<PageContent.Root variant="full">` |
| `<PageContentWide columns="1/1">` | `<PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1">` with `<PageContent.Column>` children |
| `<PageContentWide columns="2/1">` (implicit sticky right column) | `<PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">` with `<PageContent.Column sticky>` |

Key differences:

- **Single component** with a `variant` prop instead of three separate components
- **Explicit columns** via `<PageContent.Column>` instead of implicit `Children.toArray` splitting
- **Opt-in sticky** via the `sticky` prop instead of automatic sticky on the right column in `2/1` mode

## Resources

- [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-pagecontent--docs)
- [Merchant Center Page Content Layouts](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts) (predecessor)

toc.value: Migrating from Merchant Center Application Kit

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

PageContent guidelines focus on selecting the correct width constraint and
column layout for your content, ensuring visual hierarchy and a consistent
reading experience across page types and screen sizes.

### Best practices

- **Choose the right width:** Match the `variant` to the content type. Use
  `narrow` for simple forms, `wide` for complex forms or small tables, and
  `full` for large tables with many columns.
- **Limit layout types per page:** Use a maximum of two different width variants
  on a single page to maintain a consistent visual hierarchy.
- **Use explicit columns:** Always wrap column content in `PageContent.Column`
  components when using multi-column layouts (`1/1` or `2/1`). This makes the
  layout structure clear and intentional.
- **Sticky sidebar for context:** Use the `sticky` prop on sidebar columns that
  contain meta information, navigation links, or status details that should
  remain visible while the user scrolls through primary content.
- **Responsive awareness:** Multi-column layouts automatically collapse to a
  single column on smaller screens. Design content within each column to work
  well in both its multi-column and collapsed single-column states.
- **Consistent spacing:** Use the default gap of `800` (32px) between columns
  for most layouts. Only override the gap when the content demands tighter or
  wider spacing.

### Usage

PageContent provides the structural foundation for page-level layouts. The
correct layout choice depends on the type of content being displayed and the
reading patterns it supports.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Simple forms and focused content:** Use `variant="narrow"` when form fields
  should span the available width and the content is straightforward.
- **Complex forms or small tables:** Use `variant="wide"` for forms with
  multiple sections or tables with a moderate number of columns.
- **Large tables and horizontal content:** Use `variant="full"` when content has
  a strong horizontal reading flow, such as wide data tables.
- **Side-by-side content grouping:** Use `columns="1/1"` to group related
  content into equal columns, saving vertical space.
- **Main content with sidebar:** Use `columns="2/1"` for forms or content that
  benefits from adjacent meta information, links, or status indicators.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Don't use narrow for tables:** Tables generally need more horizontal space.
  Use `wide` or `full` instead.
- **Don't use full for simple forms:** Forms without many columns do not benefit
  from the full viewport width and become harder to read.
- **Don't nest PageContent within PageContent:** Each page section should use a
  single PageContent. Nesting creates unexpected layout behavior.
- **Don't use equal columns (1/1) for forms and tables:** Equal columns are best
  for grouped informational content, not for forms or tabular data.

### Choosing a width variant

Select the width variant based on the primary content type on the page.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use `narrow` for simple forms where fields span the full width. This
>   focuses the user's attention and reduces unnecessary horizontal scanning.
> - Use `wide` for forms with more complexity or small tables. Consider using
>   `horizontalConstraint` on form fields to limit their width within the wider
>   container.
> - Use `full` for large data tables that benefit from the entire viewport
>   width.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <PageContent.Root variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Narrow: Simple form</Text>
          <Text>Fields span the focused width.</Text>
          <Text color="neutral.11">The difference from wide is only visible when the viewport is wider than the narrow max-width of 3xl (768px).</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="wide">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Wide: Complex form or small table</Text>
          <Text>More room for multi-section layouts.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="full">
      <Box bg="teal.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="teal.12">Full: Large table</Text>
          <Text>Content spans the entire available width.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't use `full` width for simple forms. The excessive width makes the
>   content harder to read and creates an unfocused layout.
> - Don't use `narrow` for data tables. The constrained width clips columns and
>   forces horizontal scrolling.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <PageContent.Root variant="full">
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Full: Simple form (avoid)</Text>
          <Text>This form does not need the full viewport width. Use narrow instead.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Narrow: Data table (avoid)</Text>
          <Text>Tables need more room. Use wide or full instead.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Column layout selection

Choose the column layout based on how your content should be organized.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use `columns="2/1"` for a main content area with a sidebar for meta
>   information, related links, or status indicators.
> - Use the `sticky` prop on the sidebar column so it remains visible as the
>   user scrolls through primary content.
> - Use `columns="1/1"` to group related content side by side when both
>   sections are equally important.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200" minHeight="200px">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content</Text>
          <Text>Form fields and primary content live here.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column sticky>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sticky sidebar</Text>
          <Text>Meta information stays visible on scroll.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't use equal columns (`1/1`) for a primary content area with a secondary
>   sidebar. The visual weight should reflect the content hierarchy. Use `2/1`
>   instead.
> - Don't place tables inside multi-column layouts. Tables need the full
>   available width of a single-column layout.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="1/1">
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main form (avoid 1/1 for this)</Text>
          <Text>This content is more important than the sidebar. Use 2/1 to reflect the hierarchy.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
    <PageContent.Column>
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Stack gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sidebar</Text>
          <Text>Secondary content gets equal space unnecessarily.</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Column>
  </PageContent.Root>
)
```

### Combining layouts on a page

Multiple PageContent sections can be stacked on a single page, but limit the
number of different width variants to maintain visual consistency.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Stack multiple PageContent sections vertically for different content zones.
> - Use a maximum of two different width variants on a page.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <PageContent.Root variant="wide" columns="2/1">
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Wide: Form section</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
      <PageContent.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Sidebar</Text>
        </Box>
      </PageContent.Column>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="full">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Full: Data table section</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't use more than two different layout types on a single page. Mixing
>   narrow, wide, and full on the same page creates a disjointed visual
>   experience.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <PageContent.Root variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Narrow section</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="wide">
      <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Wide section</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>

    <PageContent.Root variant="full">
      <Box bg="teal.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text fontWeight="bold" color="teal.12">Full section (three variants is too many)</Text>
      </Box>
    </PageContent.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Predecessor

PageContent replaces the `PageContentWide`, `PageContentNarrow`, and
`PageContentFull` components from Merchant Center Application Kit. For detailed
migration instructions, see the
[Migration guide](/components/layout/pagecontent/dev#migrating-from-merchant-center-application-kit)
in the implementation docs. For the original Application Kit documentation, see
[Page Content Layouts](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/development/page-content-layouts).
